msgs.text:
## Turn 133 — the frozen (A_2) candidate must be retired

The three attached reviews expose a foundational error, not another gap in the certificate recurrences. For the specialization
[
x_0^2x_1^4[x_1,\sigma]=1,
]
Labute’s intrinsic orientation gives
[
\chi(x_0)=-1,\qquad \chi(x_1)=1,\qquad \chi(\sigma)=(-3)^{-1}.
]
Yet the claimed marking has (\nu_{\rm ur}(x_1)=0), while (\bar x_1) is an infinite-order class in
[
D^{\rm ab}\cong \mathbf Z_2^2\oplus C_2.
]
This contradicts the injectivity of
[
(\nu_{\rm ur},\chi):
G_{\mathbf Q_2}(2)^{\rm ab}\longrightarrow
\mathbf Z_2\times\mathbf Z_2^\times.
]
The precise false step is the attempted upgrade from Labute’s unmarked Nielsen normal form to one preserving an independently fixed (\mathbf Z_2)-character.  

### 1. The objection disproves the candidate, not merely Lemma 3.4

Let
[
W_A=\langle!\langle x_0,x_1\rangle!\rangle\triangleleft\Gamma_A.
]
The cofiltered construction in the history makes (W_A) pro-(2), with quotient the tame group
[
T=\langle\sigma,\tau\mid \tau^\sigma=\tau^2\rangle.
]

Uniformly,
[
O_2(T)=1.
]
Indeed, if a normal pro-(2) subgroup had Frobenius coordinate (a\in\mathbf Z_2), normality against an odd inertia quotient (C_e) would force
[
2^a\equiv1\pmod e.
]
Taking finite tame quotients with (\operatorname{ord}_e(2)=2^m) for arbitrarily large (m) forces (a\in2^m\mathbf Z_2) for every (m), hence (a=0). The subgroup would then lie in prime-to-(2) inertia and must be trivial.

Consequently,
[
W_A=O_2(\Gamma_A).
]
The identical argument gives
[
P_{\rm wild}=O_2(G_{\mathbf Q_2}).
]
Both subgroups are therefore characteristic. Any abstract isomorphism
[
\Gamma_A\simeq G_{\mathbf Q_2}
]
would send (W_A) to wild inertia and induce a maximal pro-(2) isomorphism preserving the kernel of the unramified character, up to multiplication of that character by a (2)-adic unit.

The class of (x_1) would then remain a nonzero infinite-order class satisfying
[
\nu_{\rm ur}(x_1)=0,\qquad \chi(x_1)=1,
]
which is impossible. Thus there is no proof-only repair: **the old (A_2) word itself is wrong**.

This also means that weakening the boundary from the full (\mathbf Z_2)-marking to the parity boundary used around Turn 112 does not help: local reciprocity actually forces (\nu_{\rm ur}(x_1)) to be a (2)-adic unit, so the mismatch is already visible modulo (2). The later turns silently treated an abstract Demushkin isomorphism as a marked one.  

### 2. The corrected marked pro-(2) relation

Start from Labute’s standard variables
[
D_{\rm std}
=\langle a,b,c\mid a^2b^4[b,c]=1\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}.
]
The compatible unramified marking is
[
\nu(a)=-2,\qquad \nu(b)=1,\qquad \nu(c)=0.
]
Set
[
\sigma=b,\qquad x_0=(ab^2)^{-1},\qquad x_1=c.
]
Then
[
\nu(\sigma)=1,\qquad \nu(x_0)=\nu(x_1)=0,
]
and inversion followed by cyclic conjugation transforms the standard relator into
[
\boxed{x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma]=1.}
]

Its abelianization is
[
\mathbf Z_2\bar\sigma\oplus
\mathbf Z_2\bar x_1\oplus
C_2\bar x_0,
]
correctly separating the unramified, principal-cyclotomic, and sign directions. The alternative formula appearing in one review is equivalent after changing (x_0) to (x_0^{-1}) and changing the relator by inversion and cyclic conjugacy. 

### 3. A structurally corrected trial word

Among the proposed repairs, the most promising is the (h_0)-block because it not only gives the correct marked specialization but also cancels the unwanted new first derivative.

A provisional candidate is:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
g0 = sigma2^2
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0
dg = d0^g0
hcomm = dg^-1*d0^-1*dg*d0
h0 = (x0^g0)*x0*dg*d0*d0^2*hcomm

h0*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

This is a **search candidate**, not a claimed answer.

It passes three structural gates immediately.

In the tame quotient, (x_0=x_1=1), while the (\omega _2)-powers of odd tame inertia vanish, so the displayed relation is automatic.

In a pro-(2) quotient,
[
\tau=1,\quad \sigma_2=\sigma,\quad
u_i=x_i,\quad d_0=c_0=1,
]
and the relation becomes exactly
[
x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma]=1.
]

Finally, let (P) be the tame-inertia norm projector and (U=S^{\omega _2}). For lift coordinates (b,c) on (\tau,x_0),
[
Dd_0=Pb+(P+1)c.
]
The new block has linearization
[
\begin{aligned}
Dh_0
&=(1+U^{-2})c+(1+U^{-2})Dd_0\
&=P(1+U^{-2})(b+c).
\end{aligned}
]
If (P=0), this vanishes directly. If (P=1), inertia is trivial and the (2)-primary part (U) acts trivially on every simple characteristic-(2) module, so it again vanishes. Thus the old simple-module Jacobian is preserved uniformly.

The reviews also propose that this block preserves the relevant second-order Heisenberg and extraspecial coefficients. That calculation must now be rederived directly from the exact word; it should not be inherited from the obsolete (A_2) manuscript.

### 4. Consequence for Turns 128–132

The nonnegative certificate checker and the four source-free recurrences may remain useful as abstract finite-target machinery. They cannot currently certify the claimed presentation, because their terminal values and common boundary were built from the false marked identification. Rechecking recurrence signs while retaining that boundary would only refine a conditional theorem whose hypothesis is impossible.

The correct order is now:

1. prove the marked pro-(2) specialization and orientation gate;
2. verify the full first- and second-order coefficient data for the new word;
3. test marked gluing through (C_5\rtimes C_4) and (C_{17}\rtimes C_8), coupled to characteristic quotients of the corrected marked Demushkin group;
4. rerun (GL_2(3)), tame-open duality, and the central-cover families;
5. only then reconnect the exact-image/Fourier recurrence framework and the large verifier.

**ESTABLISHED**: Accepted the reviewer contradiction and upgraded it to a structural disproof of the old (A_2) candidate using (O_2(T)=1). Derived the correct unramified-marked Demushkin relation (x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma]=1). Identified a concrete repaired (h_0)-trial word and proved that it has the correct tame and pro-(2) specializations while preserving the old simple-module first-order Jacobian.

**GAP**: The (h_0)-trial word has not yet had its mixed Heisenberg Hessian, extraspecial quadratic form, determinant/Arf data, or finite marked counts verified. Turns 128–132 remain conditional machinery over an invalid old boundary.

**NEXT**: Compute the complete second-order jet of (h_0) uniformly in the two projector branches (P=0,1); reject or retain the trial word from that structural calculation before running any individual finite target.

[BREAKTHROUGH]

title: Turn 133 — the frozen (A_2) candidate must be retired
